skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, edit, inspect, and validate PowerPoint presentations using PPTX, OOXML, HTML-to-slide, thumbnail, inventory, and replacement workflows.
skill.summary: Work with PowerPoint presentations through structured PPTX and OOXML workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many false positives from Markdown code formatting, OOXML schema vocabulary, namespace URLs, and color values. Human review did confirm high-impact archive extraction risks and legitimate external converters that process untrusted presentation files, so publication should wait for mitigation.

high_findings.title: Unsafe archive extraction of Office files
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Multiple helpers call ZipFile.extractall on user-supplied Office archives. A crafted PPTX, DOCX, or XLSX could contain traversal entries that write outside the chosen output directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly extracts archives supplied by the user without visible member path validation. This is a classic zip-slip risk and the affected files are part of the documented PPTX workflow.

medium_findings.title: External document conversion commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with limited injection risk: The skill runs LibreOffice, Poppler, and git subprocesses on presentation or temporary files. Arguments are passed as arrays, but external parsers process untrusted documents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and operate on files derived from user workflows. No shell=True or string command construction was found in these representative calls, reducing command injection confidence.

medium_findings.title: Privileged dependency installation commands in documentation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE as an operational risk: The skill documentation recommends sudo apt-get commands for LibreOffice and Poppler. This is expected setup guidance but requires elevated privileges if followed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are present in documentation, not hidden executable code. They still create a real marketplace warning because users may run them during setup.

low_findings.title: No malicious intent confirmed in scanner heuristics
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE GROUP: The critical obfuscation, C2 keyword, weak cryptography, and many Ruby backtick findings are caused by Markdown formatting, OOXML schema names, color values, and namespace URLs. No prompt injection attempt or credential exfiltration intent was found in reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed benign documentation, conversion helpers, XML schema metadata, and presentation formatting utilities. The scanner labels match substrings rather than malicious behavior in these examples.

low_findings.title: Benign environment and namespace usage
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE GROUP: The reviewed environment access selects a temporary directory, and the hardcoded URLs are OOXML namespace identifiers. These do not show secret access or outbound network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The process.env access is limited to TMPDIR fallback handling, and namespace URL constants are not fetched. No evidence found for credential exfiltration in the reviewed locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe archive extraction of Office files
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Multiple helpers call ZipFile.extractall on user-supplied Office archives. A crafted PPTX, DOCX, or XLSX could contain traversal entries that write outside the chosen output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly extracts archives supplied by the user without visible member path validation. This is a classic zip-slip risk and the affected files are part of the documented PPTX workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: External document conversion commands
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with limited injection risk: The skill runs LibreOffice, Poppler, and git subprocesses on presentation or temporary files. Arguments are passed as arrays, but external parsers process untrusted documents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and operate on files derived from user workflows. No shell=True or string command construction was found in these representative calls, reducing command injection confidence.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable help creating and updating slide decks without manually editing PPTX internals. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for presentation creation, inspection, conversion, and repair.
actual_capabilities: Create new PowerPoint decks from HTML slide layouts using a bundled html2pptx workflow.
Inspect raw PPTX XML for slides, notes, comments, layouts, themes, and media.
Unpack and repack Office documents while preserving OOXML structure.
Generate slide thumbnails through LibreOffice and Poppler conversion tools.
Inventory slide shapes and replace selected presentation content.
Validate OOXML relationships, schema structure, and presentation-specific references.
limitations: Requires external tools such as LibreOffice, Poppler, Playwright, and pptxgenjs for full functionality.
Handles PPTX internals directly, so malformed files can break workflows.
Archive extraction helpers need zip-slip hardening before public distribution.
Some advanced PowerPoint effects may not survive HTML-based generation exactly.

use_cases.title: Build a client presentation
use_cases.description: Create a polished slide deck from an outline, including titles, layouts, speaker notes, and visual hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Update an existing deck
use_cases.description: Inspect a PPTX file, locate target shapes, replace text or images, and repack the presentation.

use_cases.title: Audit presentation structure
use_cases.description: Review themes, layouts, comments, notes, relationships, and thumbnails before sharing a deck.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6 slide PowerPoint deck about [topic]. Use a clear structure, speaker notes, and a professional visual style.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an uploaded deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Inspect content and design before editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this PPTX file. Summarize the slide structure, theme colors, fonts, notes, comments, and any layout issues.

prompt_templates.title: Revise a branded presentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Edit an existing deck while preserving design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this presentation with the new messaging below. Preserve layouts, theme colors, images, and speaker notes where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and repair OOXML
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for direct PPTX internals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack this PPTX, inspect the OOXML relationships and slide references, repair structural issues, then repack and validate the file.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly business review deck from a short outline.
output_examples.output: A complete PPTX deck with an agenda, metric slides, risks, recommendations, and speaker notes.

output_examples.input: Review an existing presentation for hidden content and layout problems.
Replace placeholders in a sales deck with updated customer data.
output_examples.output: An updated presentation with targeted text replacements and the original design structure preserved.

best_practices: Work on copies of presentations before unpacking or replacing internal files.
Validate generated decks and inspect thumbnails before sending them to users.
Use trusted source files until archive extraction path checks are added.
anti_patterns: Do not process untrusted PPTX files in privileged directories.
Do not run setup commands with sudo unless the environment owner approves them.
Do not assume HTML rendering will match every PowerPoint feature exactly.

faq.question: Can this skill create new PPTX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides an HTML-to-PPTX workflow for building slide decks with positioned text, images, shapes, and placeholders.

faq.question: Can it edit existing presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can unpack PPTX files, inspect XML, update content, and repack the Office archive.

faq.question: Does it support speaker notes and comments?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented raw XML workflow covers notes slides, modern comments, layouts, themes, and media folders.

faq.question: Why is the security rating high?
faq.answer: The bundled scripts extract user-supplied Office archives without path validation. That can allow path traversal in crafted files.

faq.question: Does the skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found for active outbound requests in the reviewed files. Most URLs are OOXML namespace identifiers.

faq.question: What external dependencies are needed?
faq.answer: Full workflows may need LibreOffice, Poppler, Playwright, pptxgenjs, sharp, markitdown, and defusedxml.
